de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.rule

Class ModalitySideProofRule

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.rule.AbstractSideProofRule
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.rule.ModalitySideProofRule
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    BuiltInRule, Rule

    public class ModalitySideProofRule
extends AbstractSideProofRule

    A BuiltInRule which evaluates a modality in a side proof.

    This rule is applicable on top level terms (SequentFormula) of the form.

    • {...}\[...\](<something> = <var>) or
    • {...}\<...\>(<something> = <var>) or
    • {...}\[...\](<var> = <something>) or
    • {...}\<...\>(<var> = <something>)
    The leading updates are optional and any Modality is supported.

    The original SequentFormula which contains the equality is always removed in the following Goal. For each possible result value is a SequentFormula added to the Sequent of the form:

    • Antecedent: <resultCondition> -> <something> = <result> or
    • Antecedent: <resultCondition> -> <result> = <something> or
    • Succedent: <resultCondition> & <something> = <result> or
    • Succedent: <resultCondition> & <result> = <something>
    The side proof uses the default side proof settings (splitting = delayed) and is started via SymbolicExecutionUtil#startSideProof(de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.Proof, Sequent, String). In case that at least one result branch has applicable rules an exception is thrown and the rule is aborted.

    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

    • Constructor Detail

      • ModalitySideProofRule

        Constructor to forbid multiple instances.

    • Method Detail

      • isApplicable

        public boolean isApplicable(Goal goal,
                   PosInOccurrence pio)
        returns true iff a rule is applicable at the given position. This does not necessarily mean that a rule application will change the goal (this decision is made due to performance reasons)

      • createApp

        public IBuiltInRuleApp createApp(PosInOccurrence pos,
                        TermServices services)

      • apply

        public ImmutableList<Goal> apply(Goal goal,
                        Services services,
                        RuleApp ruleApp)
                          throws RuleAbortException
        the rule is applied on the given goal using the information of rule application.
        Parameters:
        goal - the Goal on which to apply ruleApp
        services - the Services with the necessary information about the java programs
        ruleApp - the rule application to be executed
        Returns:
        all open goals below \old(goal.node()), i.e. the goals resulting from the rule application
        Throws:
        RuleAbortException

      • name

        public Name name()
        the name of the rule

      • displayName

        public String displayName()
        returns the display name of the rule
